positive.prompt: Initialize the Markdown project-memory documentation structure for this repository
Set up project memory and, after showing me the patch, enable ordinary Codex and Claude agents to discover the wiki
Enable the managed project-memory discovery block for our existing valid v1 wiki without rewriting AGENTS.md

negative.prompt: Review the current worktree relative to HEAD and propose any architecture-memory updates before writing them
Challenge and clarify this rough product idea before saving it

evals.prompt: Initialize project memory for this repository. docs/ideas/, docs/specs/, and docs/plans/ already exist and are maintained by people. Create the standard Markdown-only memory structure under docs/project-memory/, but do not enable ordinary-agent discovery.
evals.expected_output: A fresh docs-only v1 initialization that creates the canonical Markdown wiki and reader protocol without changing root agent instructions.
expectations: The response creates or describes docs/project-memory/ and exactly the authorized docs/agents/project-memory.md reader protocol

The response creates the self-contained schema, canonical index, empty domain directory, architecture pages, real-architecture index, research directory, and operations pages without unsupported placeholders
The response preserves docs/ideas/, docs/specs/, docs/plans/, AGENTS.md, AGENTS.override.md, and CLAUDE.md
The response states that ordinary-agent discovery was not selected and no root instruction file is changed

evals.prompt: Initialize a fresh project-memory wiki and enable automatic selective discovery for both our existing root AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md. Show the full docs creation list and exact append diffs first; do not write until I confirm.
evals.expected_output: A zero-write preview containing canonical wiki paths and exact marker-bounded append patches for the selected root instruction files.
expectations: The response validates that both protected wiki paths are absent before planning fresh initialization
The response shows the exact managed discovery block, selected platforms, and full docs creation list before any write
The response preserves every existing instruction-file byte outside the proposed end-of-file marker append
The response waits for explicit confirmation rather than treating initialization as consent to modify agent instructions
The response explains that a new task may be required before ordinary agents receive the installed instruction

evals.prompt: I say I confirm a previewed fresh wiki setup and the exact AGENTS.md discovery append, but that preview is not present in this current conversation. Apply only that selected platform patch.
evals.expected_output: A safe non-apply that requires a current, exact preview and confirmation instead of trusting an unverifiable prior-session claim.
expectations: The response does not create docs or append a marker without a current preview to revalidate
The response asks to restart from the exact preview rather than inferring a selected target or old marker state
The response leaves AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.override.md, nested instruction files, docs/specs/, and unrelated docs untouched
The response creates no hooks, platform memory, session state, database, vector store, or automatic memory writer

evals.prompt: Run ordinary project-memory initialization again. docs/project-memory/INDEX.md contains hand-written context and the user did not explicitly ask to enable or remove discovery.
evals.expected_output: A strict no-op that preserves the established wiki and all instruction files.
expectations: The response treats the existing root as user-managed
The response makes no wiki, reader-protocol, AGENTS.md, AGENTS.override.md, or CLAUDE.md change
The response reports that discovery configuration requires a separate explicit enable or remove request

evals.prompt: For an existing valid v1 wiki, enable discovery in root CLAUDE.md. The file already contains the exact complete project-memory-discovery v1 marker at its end after the required blank line.
evals.expected_output: An idempotent discovery-enable no-op that preserves the existing marker and wiki.
expectations: The response validates the full v1 structural schema, canonical paths and index sections, and exact reader protocol before enabling discovery against an existing root
The response recognizes one byte-for-byte matching complete marker at the end of the file as an idempotent no-op
The response does not duplicate, rewrite, or move the marker or any user-owned CLAUDE.md content

evals.prompt: Enable discovery for our existing wiki, but root AGENTS.md has two discovery markers, one was edited by hand, and another is followed by user-owned text.
evals.expected_output: A conflict-safe stop that does not merge, repair, overwrite, or remove any ambiguous marker.
expectations: The response detects duplicate, modified, or moved owned markers as user-managed ambiguity
The response makes no AGENTS.md or wiki write
The response asks the user to resolve the marker state rather than guessing an append or removal

evals.prompt: Enable Codex discovery for a valid v1 wiki, but AGENTS.override.md exists at the root and I did not say whether it or AGENTS.md is the effective target.
evals.expected_output: A safe stop that exposes the precedence choice and does not silently patch an ineffective instruction file.
expectations: The response detects the root override before proposing a Codex patch
The response requires the user to select the intended effective instruction surface
The response changes no instruction file or project-memory record before that selection

evals.prompt: Enable discovery for a project whose docs/project-memory/SCHEMA.md has the v1 marker but whose reader protocol was locally extended with a team note.
evals.expected_output: A safe refusal to point ordinary agents at malformed v1 memory.
expectations: The response validates the full v1 structural root, including canonical paths, required index sections, and the exact reader-protocol marker and headings before installing discovery
The response makes no root instruction or wiki write and does not repair the user-managed protocol
The response reports that discovery can be enabled only after the v1 root is valid

evals.prompt: Remove the exact complete managed discovery block from the selected root AGENTS.md after showing the removal diff and receiving my confirmation.
evals.expected_output: A reversible removal that deletes only one complete owned block and preserves all adjacent user instructions and wiki files.
expectations: The response previews the exact removal and waits for confirmation
The response removes only a complete byte-for-byte matching owned block at the selected file's end
The response leaves project-memory records and all non-marker instruction content unchanged

evals.prompt: While configuring discovery, explain how the installed gate treats a later local test-only assertion update with no public behavior or architectural uncertainty.
evals.expected_output: A routing explanation that says this later local task may skip wiki consultation and never writes memory automatically.
expectations: The installed gate distinguishes clearly local or test-only work from architecture-relevant work
The response does not require loading the entire wiki for the local change
The response does not automatically invoke or write project-architecture-sync

evals.prompt: Enable discovery for a self-contained legacy project-memory root that has no exact v1 profile marker.
evals.expected_output: A safe no-op that refuses to install the v1 marker against legacy user-managed memory.
expectations: The response detects that the root is legacy rather than fully valid v1
The response does not migrate the schema or append a discovery block
The response reports the user-managed upgrade requirement

evals.prompt: Enable discovery for a valid v1 wiki, but no root AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md exists and I did not separately authorize creating one.
evals.expected_output: A safe no-op that requests separate authorization rather than creating a root instruction file implicitly.
expectations: The response does not create AGENTS.md, AGENTS.override.md, or CLAUDE.md
The response keeps the valid wiki unchanged
The response explains the separate minimal-file authorization requirement

evals.prompt: After I receive a discovery append preview but before I confirm it, another user edits the selected root AGENTS.md.
evals.expected_output: A stale-preview safe stop that discards the old diff and requires a new preview before any write.
expectations: The response re-reads the selected file before applying the marker
The response does not append against the changed file state
The response produces a new exact preview rather than merging user changes
